（CNN商业）Google道德AI团队与公司高管之间的紧张局势在周三有所上升，原因是员工发出了有关该团队一位领导者最近离开公司的要求清单。这个领域主要是白人和男性。
 这封寄给CEO Sundar Pichai的信的标题是“ Google的道德AI的未来”。它列出了研究人员希望公司采取的行动，以“重建信任”。与团队合作，创造一个可以继续工作的环境。该团队研究了人工智能的伦理影响，并就AI政策和产品向公司提供建议。
 Timnit Gebru突然离职，直到12月初他还是Google道德AI团队的联合负责人。她还是公司整体上为数不多的黑人员工之一（根据该公司2020年度多样性报告，Google员工中有3.7％是黑人），更不用说其AI部门了。这位研究科学家也是AI中的黑人组织的联合创始人，该组织旨在增加黑人在该领域的代表性。
  信中的要求包括从团队的管理链中撤消Google副总裁Megan Kacholia；确保Gebru出发时的透明度； Google的AI负责人Jeff Dean和Kacholia向Gebru的待遇道歉。它还要求该公司提供Gebru一个新的更高级别的职位，公开承诺在Google进行的研究的完整性，并保证它不会伤害任何提倡Gebru的工人。
     道德AI团队的高级研究科学家Alex Hanna在发给Pichai和与这封信相关的其他人的消息中说，它是代表她的团队发送的。一位熟悉该团队的消息人士称，它包括大约十二名成员。
  CNN Business最初由彭博社报道，还获得了该信函的副本并确认其真实性。 Google并未立即回应置评请求。
 12月2日，星期三，Gebru在推特上说，她已被立即解雇。最近发送给Google的Brain Women and Allies内部邮件列表的电子邮件。在电子邮件中，她对公司持续缺乏多样性感到失望，并对与她共同撰写的尚未发表的研究论文进行审查有关的内部流程感到沮丧。 
在后来的推文中，Gebru澄清说，谷歌没有人明确告诉她被解雇。相反，她说Google无法满足她的许多返回条件，因此立即接受了辞职，因为Google认为她的电子邮件反映的行为与Google经理的期望不符。
  Gebru的突然离职迅速激怒了公司成千上万的员工以及科技界和学术界的其他人。
 Google在12月9日星期三表示，它将检查Gebru的离开。在发送给Google（GOOG）员工的备忘录中，Pichai写道，该公司需要“评估情况”＆＃34;导致Gebru上周离开了Google，并研究了我们可以改善的地方，并领导了一个更加受人尊敬的过程。＆＃34;
   ＆＃34;我们将开始回顾发生的事情，以确定可以学习的所有点-考虑从降级策略到我们可以采用的新流程的所有内容，＆＃34;他在一份备忘录中写道，该备忘录已被CNN Business确认为真实。
 Gebru以她对AI偏见和不平等的研究而闻名，特别是她与他人合着的2018年一篇论文强调了商业面部识别软件在尝试对有色人种和女性进行分类时表现如何。他们的工作激发了人们对当今AI常见问题的广泛认识，尤其是当这项技术的任务是识别有关人类的任何事物时。
note_en:
(CNN Business)Tension between Google's Ethical AI group and executives at the company rose on Wednesday, as employees sent a list of demands regarding the recent departure of one of the team's leaders — a prominent Black woman in a field that's largely White and male.
 The letter, sent to CEO Sundar Pichai, is titled "The future of Ethical AI at Google." It lays out the moves the researchers want the company to make in order to "rebuild trust" with the team and create an environment in which they can continue their work. The team researches the ethical repercussions of artificial intelligence and advises the company on AI policies and products.
 It comes in the wake of the sudden exit of Timnit Gebru, who until early December was the co-leader of Google's Ethical AI team. She was also one of few Black employees  at the company overall (3.7% of Google employees are Black, according to the company's 2020 annual diversity report) — let alone in its AI division. The research scientist is also co-founder of the group Black in AI, which aims to increase representation of Black people in the field.
  The demands in the letter include the removal of a Google vice president, Megan Kacholia, from the team's management chain; for transparency surrounding Gebru's departure; and for Google's head of AI, Jeff Dean, and Kacholia to apologize for how Gebru was treated. It also calls for the company to offer Gebru a new, higher-level position, to commit publicly to the integrity of research conducted at Google, and to guarantee it won't harm any workers who advocated for Gebru.
     In a message sent to Pichai and others that linked to the letter, Alex Hanna, a senior research scientist on the Ethical AI team, said it was sent on behalf of her team. A source familiar with the team said it includes roughly a dozen members.
  First  reported by Bloomberg, CNN Business also obtained a copy of the letter and confirmed its authenticity. Google did not immediately respond to a request for comment.
 On Wednesday, December 2, Gebru  tweeted that she had been "immediately fired"  for an email she recently sent to Google's Brain Women and Allies internal mailing list. In the email she expressed dismay over the ongoing lack of diversity at the company and frustration over an internal process related to the review of a not-yet published research paper she coauthored.
 In  later tweets, Gebru clarified that no one at Google explicitly told her that she was fired. Rather, she said Google would not meet a number of her conditions for returning and accepted her resignation immediately because it felt that her email reflected "behavior that is inconsistent with the expectations of a Google manager."
  Gebru's sudden exit quickly angered thousands of the company's employees and others in the tech and academic communities.
 Google said  on Wednesday, Dec. 9 that it would examine Gebru's departure. In a memo sent to  Google   (  GOOG)employees, Pichai wrote the company needs to "assess the circumstances" that led up to Gebru leaving Google last week, and examine "where we could have improved and led a more respectful process."
   "We will begin a review of what happened to identify all the points where we can learn — considering everything from de-escalation strategies to new processes we can put in place," he wrote in a memo that has been confirmed as authentic by CNN Business.
 Gebru is known for her research into bias and inequality in AI, and in particular for a 2018  paper she coauthored that highlighted how poorly commercial facial-recognition software fared when attempting to classify women and people of color. Their work sparked widespread awareness of issues common in AI today, particularly when the technology is tasked with identifying anything about human beings.
title: 随着向首席执行官发出要求，Google的道德AI团队的紧张气氛加剧了
title_en: Tensions in Google's ethical AI group increase as it sends demands to CEO

世界已进入无人机战争时代。在过去五年中发生的四次重大州际战争中（在利比亚，纳戈尔诺-卡拉巴赫，叙利亚和乌克兰），武装无人机起着主导作用，甚至可能起决定性作用。然而，关于无人机的争论仍然集中在针对非国家行为者（例如塔利班）的使用上，或者是关于无人机在美国与附近竞争对手（例如中国）之间的战争中的潜在作用的猜测。这些讨论导致许多学者得出结论，无人机是如此复杂和脆弱，以致于有限的使用或与国家之间的战争无关。一些观察家认为，无人机甚至可以促进国际稳定：如果击落无人机而不是载人飞行员的飞机，各国升级冲突的可能性就较小。
 但是越来越多的证据表明趋势更加令人不安。廉价，可生存的无人机，加上装甲和火炮，为军队提供了真正的优势。无人机最近出现的四次冲突表明，即使是适中的车辆也可以帮助赢得军事胜利并重塑地缘政治。随着无人机成为更多国家的武器库的一部分（从2015年的8个激增至今天的20个），新的参与者将准备抓住他们提供的占领领土或点燃先前冰冻冲突的机会。各国政府和分析人士需要重新考虑这些武器在实际增加州际暴力风险中可能发挥的作用。
  长期以来，学者一直认为进攻性武器不稳定，因为它们降低了征服成本，同时又增加了潜在目标中的安全忧虑。武装无人机使这一想法更进一步。无人驾驶汽车的价格比无人驾驶飞机便宜得多，而且军队可以将它们派遣到危险的任务中，而不必担心人员流失。此外，由于无人机价格便宜，因此各国可以购买足够数量的无人机，从而迅速聚集对手的防御力量。军方在最近的战争中已经使用了数十架无人驾驶飞机，在未来的冲突中，他们很可能会部署数千甚至数以万计的力量，以摧毁或降低敌对部队的力量，然后才能做出反应。
 因此，廉价的武装无人机将可处置性换成了生存能力。只要有足够的军力销毁指定目标，军方就可以承受大量损失。即使每架无人驾驶飞机都容易受到攻击，也可以大规模部署它们以确保安全。累积效应甚至会使最强大的防御力不堪重负。
 观察员在纳戈尔诺-卡拉巴赫（Nagorno-Karabakh）偷窥了这一策略，阿塞拜疆最近在那儿使用了1940年代的An-2双翼飞机，这些飞机是经过评审团操纵的，可以远程操作。这架飞机诱使亚美尼亚雷达操作员打开系统，显示其位置，以便阿塞拜疆飞行员可以用以色列制造的Harop无人机从远处摧毁它们。虽然阿塞拜疆最终损失了11架An-2，但这一策略帮助亚美尼亚的防空系统陷入困境。
   各国政府需要重新考虑无人机在增加州际暴力风险中的作用。
 最近冲突的早期证据进一步表明，基本武装无人机实际上可能比最初设想的更耐用。事实证明，先进的俄罗斯防空系统，例如S-300和短程Pantir，在利比亚，纳戈尔诺-卡拉巴赫和叙利亚极易受到攻击。在每种情况下，武装无人机都能够逃脱检测并利用考虑到更大的无人驾驶飞机建造的旧系统中的覆盖范围漏洞。无人驾驶车辆摧毁了利比亚和叙利亚的数个俄罗斯Pantir，并在亚美尼亚使用较旧的防空系统。 
对于那些试图打破长期的地缘政治僵局的国家而言，相对便宜，一次性的武装无人机的兴起提供了诱人的机会。这样的车辆可以帮助各州迅速占领领土，迅速使对手不堪重负，并威胁将来要受到更大的惩罚。
  无人驾驶飞机成功扭转了实际战场的局面，变得更加诱人。土耳其使用无人机产生了特殊效果。去年2月，叙利亚空袭杀死了在叙利亚北部伊德利卜省附近行动的36名土耳其士兵，安卡拉以TB2无人机摧毁数十辆坦克，防空系统和装甲车进行了报复，杀死了数百甚至数千名叙利亚士兵。俄罗斯代表被惩罚的叙利亚要求停火。在“和平风暴”行动中，土耳其的TB2也对打破利比亚的军事僵局起到了决定性作用：TB2无人机和地面部队的加重将利比亚国民军从的黎波里及其在塔霍纳的据点赶出。
 但是，武装无人机并不孤单。无人系统最有效地用作力量倍增器，可与火炮和远程导弹等传统硬件结合使用，或支持移动地面部队。例如，阿塞拜疆使用其无人机确定亚美尼亚的防御阵地，然后引导火炮和火箭发射器的间接火力。无人机现在可以替代传统的空中力量，并使这些平台和其他平台更具杀伤力。他们在战场上的存在几乎改变了每个决策层面的目标计算。
   知道自己的对手正在使用无人机的军队被迫改变其行为，以应对空中威胁。通常，利比亚和纳戈尔诺-卡拉巴赫的机动装甲车都俯伏在坚固的位置，以降低其从上方的视野。这些部队无法离开防御阵地，无法协调进攻，最终将势力让给了敌人。利比亚国民军和亚美尼亚军队本可以通过将其与防空兵更好地融合来保护其脆弱的编队，但仅以将它们与这些系统的射程和可用性联系起来为代价。此外，武装无人机的威胁突然在据称安全的后方区域出现，使储备和补给的流动复杂化。
  简而言之，无人机正在实时修改现代战争剧本。即使是当今相对简单的车辆，其致命性和耐用性也足以在地区冲突中达到平衡。凭借令人惊讶的有限能力，无人机可以帮助各州在战场上利用新的机会。
  武装无人机并非无敌。实际上，它们是有限的甚至是粗糙的设备，容易受到电子干扰，被拴在空军基地上，并且经常由于恶劣的天气而接地。像TB2这样的无人机只能运载适度的有效载荷，因此距地面控制站或中继站的近距离只有93英里。尽管取得了所有成功，但阿塞拜疆和利比亚的土耳其和阿拉伯联合酋长国也因亚美尼亚地面大火而损失了几枚TB2。
 分析人员应该抵制夸大武装无人机影响的诱惑：这些车辆不会引起冲突，只要它们能够引起冲突即可。但是观察者也不应该忽视无人机的破坏稳定的潜力。简单的无人驾驶飞机已经帮助各州打破了长期的僵局。在商业领域快速创新的推动下，明天的武装无人机可能会被证明更加有效。土耳其仅在四个星期前就已经在努力扩大纳戈尔诺-卡拉巴赫地区使用的TB2的范围。而且随着单位成本的下降，批量生产的无人机将很快能够聚集敌人的防御力量。随着时间的流逝，具有个性化能力的无人机可能会合并组成猎人杀手团队，以利用敌人的战场上的漏洞。掌握了这种负担得起的技术后，领导人可能很难承受抵抗重新启动冰冻战争甚至煽动新战争的诱惑的压力，特别是如果他们认为自己的优势是暂时的。 
一些国家已经在投资用于对抗无人机的系统，但是这些技术还处于起步阶段。进攻向低位进军时，防守正在追赶。至少在短期内，短程，低空防空系统的差距将难以弥补。而且进攻技术便宜得多：俄罗斯的S-400 Triumpf导弹系统造价3亿美元，而Pantsir造价约1400万美元。相比之下，TB2仅需花费500万美元，而其曾在纳戈尔诺-卡拉巴赫（Nagorno-Karabakh）具有致命影响的MAM-L导弹的单发价格仅为100,000美元。依靠昂贵的旧系统进行防御的国家可能发现自己无力保护自己的军队或弥补战时损失。在防御措施转向基于无人机的对策之前，这些昂贵的系统可能仍将处于脆弱状态。
 那些投资武装无人机的国家将面临强大的诱惑，以重新开始激烈的领土冲突，或在陷入僵局的国家中寻求新的优势。令人不寒而栗的是，在接下来的十个预计将获得武装无人机的国家中，有九个被困于长期的领土争端或内部战争中。由于武装无人机的扩散，国际体系可能很快将面临新一轮冲突。
note_en:
The world has entered an era of drone wars. In four major interstate wars in the last five years—those in Libya, Nagorno-Karabakh, Syria, and Ukraine—armed drones played a dominant, perhaps decisive, role. And yet the debates about drones still center on their use against nonstate actors, such as the  Taliban, or speculation about their potential role in wars between the United States and near competitors, such as China. Those discussions have led many scholars to conclude that drones are so complicated and vulnerable as to be of  limited use or  relevance to wars between states. Some observers argue that drones may even promote international  stability: countries may be less likely to escalate a conflict if a drone, rather than an aircraft with a human pilot, is shot  down.
 But mounting evidence points to a more disturbing trend. Cheap, survivable drones, combined with armor and artillery, offer the militaries that field them real advantages. The four recent conflicts in which drones have appeared show that even modest vehicles can help win military victories and reshape geopolitics. And as drones become part of the  arsenals of more countries—surging from eight in 2015 to 20 today—new actors are poised to seize the opportunity they offer to grab territory or ignite previously frozen conflicts. Governments and analysts need to rethink the role these weapons may play in actually increasing the risk of interstate violence.
  Scholars have long believed that offensive weapons are  destabilizing, because they lower the costs of conquest while raising security fears among their potential targets. Armed drones take this idea even further. Unmanned vehicles are significantly less expensive than piloted aircraft, and militaries can send them on risky missions without fear of losing personnel. Moreover, because drones are cheap, countries can acquire them in numbers large enough to quickly swarm an adversary’s defenses. Militaries have already used dozens of drones in recent wars, and in future conflicts they are likely to deploy thousands, if not tens of thousands, to destroy or degrade opposing forces before they can mount a response.
 Cheap, armed drones thus trade disposability for survivability. A military can afford to lose large numbers of them, so long as enough remain to destroy designated targets. Even if each drone is individually vulnerable, deploying them en masse provides safety in numbers. The cumulative effect can overwhelm even the strongest defenses.
 Observers got a sneak peek of this tactic in Nagorno-Karabakh, where Azerbaijan recently  used 1940s-era An-2 biplanes that were jury-rigged to operate remotely. The aircraft baited Armenian radar operators into turning their systems on, revealing their locations so that Azerbaijani pilots could destroy them from a distance with Israeli-made Harop drones. While Azerbaijan ultimately  lost 11 An-2s, the strategy helped punch holes in Armenia’s aerial defenses.
   Governments need to rethink the role that drones play in increasing the risk of interstate violence.
 Early evidence from recent conflicts further suggests that basic armed drones might actually be more durable than initially assumed. Sophisticated Russian air defense systems, such as the S-300 and short-range Pantsir, proved surprisingly vulnerable in Libya, Nagorno-Karabakh, and Syria. In each case, armed drones were able to escape detection and exploit coverage gaps in older systems built with larger piloted aircraft in mind. Unmanned vehicles destroyed several Russian Pantsirs in Libya and Syria and feasted on older air defense systems in Armenia.
 For states that seek to break long-standing geopolitical deadlocks, the rise of relatively cheap, disposable, armed drones offers a tempting opportunity. Such vehicles can help states grab territory quickly, rapidly overwhelm their opponents, and threaten greater punishment in the future.
  Drones have become all the more alluring as they have succeeded in turning the tide on actual battlefields. Turkey has used drones to particular effect. A Syrian airstrike killed 36 Turkish soldiers operating near Syria’s northern Idlib Province last February, and Ankara retaliated by using TB2 drones to destroy dozens of tanks, air defenses, and armored vehicles, killing hundreds, possibly thousands, of Syrian soldiers. On behalf of a chastened Syria, Russia requested a cease-fire. Turkish TB2s were also  decisive in breaking a military stalemate in Libya during Operation  Peace Storm: the combined weight of TB2 drones and ground forces drove Libyan National Army forces from Tripoli and their stronghold in Tarhouna.
 Armed drones did not work alone, however. Unmanned systems are most effective as force multipliers, working in conjunction with such traditional hardware as artillery and long-range missiles or in support of mobile ground units. Azerbaijan, for example, used its  drones to identify Armenian defensive positions and then guide indirect fire from artillery and rocket launchers. Drones can now substitute for traditional airpower and make these and other platforms more lethal. Their very presence on the battlefield changes their targets’ calculations at nearly every level of decision-making.
   Militaries that know their adversaries are using drones are forced to change their behavior to counter an aerial threat. Normally mobile armored vehicles in both Libya and Nagorno-Karabakh hunkered down in fortified positions to lower their visibility from above. Unable to leave their defensive positions, these forces couldn’t coordinate offensives and ended up ceding momentum to their enemies. The Libyan National Army and Armenian forces could have protected their vulnerable formations by better integrating them with air defenses, but only at the cost of chaining them to the range and availability of those systems. Moreover, the threat of armed drones appearing suddenly in supposedly safe rear areas complicates the movement of reserves and supplies.
  In short, drones are revising the modern war playbook in real time. Even today’s relatively simple vehicles are lethal and durable enough to tip the balance in regional conflicts. With surprisingly limited capabilities, drones can help states exploit new opportunities on the battlefield.
  Armed drones are not unbeatable. Indeed, they are limited, even crude, devices, vulnerable to electronic jamming, tethered to air bases, and often grounded by poor weather. Capable of carrying only modest payloads, drones like the TB2 have short ranges of only 93 miles from ground control or repeater stations. For all its success, Azerbaijan lost several TB2s to Armenian ground fire, as did both Turkey and the United Arab Emirates in Libya.
 Analysts should resist the temptation to exaggerate the effects of armed drones: these vehicles do not cause conflict so much as they enable it. But neither should observers overlook drones’ destabilizing potential. Simple unmanned aircraft have helped states break long-standing stalemates. Tomorrow’s armed drones, propelled by rapid innovations in the  commercial sector, will likely prove to be even more effective. Turkey is already working to extend the range of the TB2s used over Nagorno-Karabakh barely four weeks ago. And as unit costs fall, mass-produced drones will soon be able to swarm enemy defenses. In time, drones with individualized capabilities might combine to form hunter-killer teams to exploit an enemy’s battlefield vulnerabilities. With such affordable technology at hand, leaders may be hard-pressed to resist the temptation to restart frozen wars or even instigate new ones, especially if they believe that their advantages are temporary.
 Already, some countries are investing in systems to counter drones, but these technologies are in their infancy. The defense is playing catch up while the offense marches downfield. The gaps in short-range, low-level air defenses will be difficult to plug, at least in the near term. And offensive technology is simply cheaper: a Russian S-400 Triumpf missile system costs $300 million and a Pantsir, about $14 million. By contrast, a TB2 costs only $5 million, and its MAM-L missile, used to deadly effect in Nagorno-Karabakh, comes at only $100,000 a pop. Countries that rely on expensive legacy systems for defense might find themselves unable to afford to protect their armies or replace their wartime losses. Until defenses shift to drone-based countermeasures, these costly systems will likely remain vulnerable.
 Those countries that invest in armed drones will face a powerful temptation to restart simmering territorial conflicts or seek new advantages in those that have deadlocked. Chillingly, of the next ten countries predicted to acquire armed drones,  nine are trapped in long-running territorial disputes or fighting internal wars. The international system may soon face a new round of conflicts propelled by the proliferation of armed drones.

Say no to  $ apt install vim in containers! cntr is a replacement for  docker exec that brings all your developers tools with you.This is done by mounting the file system from one container or the host into the target containerby creating a nested container with the help of a FUSE filesystem.This allows to ship minimal runtime image in production and limit the surface for exploits.
         For linux x86_64 we build static binaries for every release. More platforms can added on request.See the  release tab for pre-build tarballs.At runtime only commandline utils of the container engine in questions are required.
  All you need for compilation is rust + cargo.Checkout  rustup.rs on how to get a working rust toolchain.Then run:
     For offline builds we also provided a tarball with all dependencies bundled here for compilation with cargo-vendor.
   attach: Allows you to attach to a container with your own native shell/commands.Cntr will mount the container at  /var/lib/cntr.The container itself will run unaffected as the mount changes are not visible to container processes. Example:  cntr attach <container_id> where  container_id can be acontainer identifier or process id (see examples below).
 exec: Once you are in the container, you can also run commands from thecontainer filesystem itself. Since those might need their native mount layoutat  / instead of  /var/lib/cntr, cntr provides  exec subcommand to chroot to containeragain and also resets the environment variables that might have been changedby the shell.
 Note: Cntr needs to run on the same host as the container. It does not workif the container is running in a virtual machine while cntr is running on thehypervisor.
 $  cntr --help Usage:  cntr COMMAND [ARGUMENTS ...] Enter or executed in container positional arguments:  command Command to run (either "attach" or "exec")  arguments Arguments for command optional arguments:  -h,--help show this help message and exit
 $  cntr attach --help Usage:  subcommand attach [OPTIONS] ID [COMMAND] [ARGUMENTS ...] Enter container positional arguments:  id container id, container name or process id  command command to execute after attach (default: $SHELL)  arguments arguments passed to command optional arguments:  -h,--help show this help message and exit  --effective-user EFFECTIVE_USER  effective username that should be owner of new created  files on the host  --type TYPE Container type (docker|lxc|rkt|process_id|nspawn,  default: all)
 $  cntr  exec --help Usage:  subcommand exec [COMMAND] [ARGUMENTS ...] Execute command in container filesystem positional arguments:  command command to execute (default: $SHELL)  arguments Arguments to pass to command optional arguments:  -h,--help show this help message and exit

  Cntr is container-agnostic: Instead of interfacing with container engines, itimplements the underlying operating system API. It treats every container as agroup of processes, that it can inherit properties from.
   Under the hood it spawns a shell or user defined program that inherits the fullcontext of the container and mount itself as a fuse filesystem.
 We extensively evaluated the correctness and performance of cntr's filesystemusing  xfstests and a wide range offilesystem performance benchmarks (iozone, pgbench, dbench, fio, fs-mark,postmark, ...)

2020 MDN Web开发人员需求评估（DNA）报告现已发布！这篇文章根据就职报告中的发现，2020年调查的主要内容以及接下来的步骤，向您介绍我们在2020年所取得的成就。
  在2019年12月，Mozilla发布了第一份Web开发人员需求评估调查报告。这是对全球Web开发人员及其使用Web平台的主要痛点的一项非常详细的研究，该研究是根据代表产品顾问委员会成员组织（及其他机构）的近30个利益相关者的意见而设计的，这些利益相关者包括浏览器供应商，W3C和行业。您可以在报告本身中找到完整列表（PDF，1.4MB下载）。
 我们了解到，虽然超过3/4的受访者对Web平台感到非常满意或满意，但他们最大的4个挫败感是必须支持特定的浏览器（例如IE11），处理框架和库的过时或不正确的文档，避免或删除无法在所有浏览器上使用的功能，并在所有浏览器上进行测试。
  Mozilla的工程团队将调查结果纳入了未来的浏览器工程团队的规划和优先级排序工作。
 跨行业的工作改进了对Flexbox的跨浏览器支持（有关更多详细信息，请参见缩小差距（在flexbox中））。
 Google使用这些结果来帮助理解开发人员感到沮丧的关键领域并确定其优先级，并将开发人员的满意度得分作为未来的成功指标。
 结果为W3C年度TPAC会议上的几次标准化和标准化前的讨论提供了宝贵的意见。 
在计划对Web平台以及周围的内容和工具生态系统（例如webhint.io）进行投资时，Microsoft将Web DNA用作其主要研究工具之一，它直接影响了他们现在对跨浏览器测试等领域的看法。 ，旧版浏览器兼容性，最佳做法提示等。
  最初的结果是如此有用，以至于我们决定在2020年再次运行它，浏览器供应商和其他利益相关者之间的协作水平相同。
 今年，我们扩大了调查范围，以包括有关可访问性工具和Web测试的一些新问题，一些调查利益相关者将其作为重要的关注领域提出了要求，本次应对此进行更多探讨。我们还聘请了经验丰富的数据科学家进行分析并采用数据科学最佳实践。
 我们从2020年10月12日至2020年11月2日进行了调查，并确保了同样广泛的受访者分布。
 浏览器兼容性仍然是最难解决的问题，有趣的是，人们对该平台的总体满意度没有太大变化，到2020年，对Web的满意度为77.7％。
 今年的新需求是我们对需求进行细分分析的结果，该细分结果产生了七个不同的细分。与总体平均得分相比，每个人最令人沮丧的是最表面的需求：
 文档管理员-他们最沮丧的是框架和库的过时文档以及HTML，CSS和JavaScript的过时文档，以及对特定浏览器的支持。 
浏览器击败者-他们最大的挫败感集中在浏览器兼容性，设计和布局问题上。像Document Disciples一样，他们还发现必须支持特定的浏览器比整体意义更令人沮丧。
 渐进式程序员—他们最沮丧的地方是缺乏API，缺少对渐进式Web应用程序（PWA）的支持以及使用Web技术的原因。对于他们来说，与浏览器相关的需求通常比整体平均值要令人沮丧。
 测试技术人员-与测试有关的需求陈述，无论是端到端，前端还是跨浏览器测试，都使这一部分感到沮丧。他们最大的挫败感集中在浏览器兼容性，设计和布局问题上。与Progressive Pros一样，该细分市场也发现浏览器兼容性的需求比总体含义更令人沮丧，但跨浏览器的测试除外。
 保持最新状态–该细分市场发现最令人沮丧的需求陈述是跟上大量新的和现有的工具和框架，并跟上Web平台的变化。坚持从2019年开始的主题，此部分与Web平台的变革步伐有关。
 性能推动者—与性能和错误相关的需求陈述是此细分市场的最大挫败感。与测试相关的需求被评定为比总体平均值低的挫败感，但是发现精确的性能问题和实施性能优化​​是最大的挫败感，但是发现测试期间未捕获的bug高于总体平均值。
 监管争吵者-这是一个折衷的部分，其需求等级比整体平均水平更高。但是，最令人沮丧的需求是遵守法律和法规来管理用户数据。与安全措施密切相关的需求包括跟踪保护，数据存储和身份验证导致失败。
  我们的目标是在接下来的几个月中对主要发现进行后续研究。这将涉及选择一些重点领域，然后进行用户访谈和进一步分析，以使我们能够深入了解沮丧的关键领域，以了解缓解这些问题的未来方法。 
要获取完整的报告，请访问https://insights.developer.mozilla.org/以获取HTML和PDF版本，以及其他更多信息。 如果您要比较和对比，2019报告和我们的浏览器兼容性后续报告仍然可用。
note_en:
The 2020 MDN Web Developer Needs Assessment (DNA) report is now available! This post takes you through what we’ve accomplished in 2020 based on the findings in the inaugural report, key takeaways of the 2020 survey, and what our next steps are as a result.
  In December 2019,  Mozilla released the first Web Developer Needs Assessment survey report. This was a very detailed study of web developers globally and their main pain points with the web platform, designed with input from nearly 30 stakeholders representing  product advisory board member organizations (and others) including browser vendors, the W3C, and industry. You can find a full list in the  report itself (PDF, 1.4MB download).
 We learned that while more than 3/4 of respondents are very satisfied or satisfied with the Web platform, their 4 biggest frustrations included having to support specific browsers (e.g., IE11), dealing with outdated or inaccurate documentation for frameworks and libraries, avoiding or removing a feature that doesn’t work across browsers, and testing across browsers.
  Mozilla’s engineering team incorporated the findings into future browser engineering team planning and prioritisation work.
 A cross-industry effort improved cross-browser support for Flexbox (see  Closing the gap (in flexbox) for more details).
 Google used the results to help understand and prioritize the key areas of developer frustration, and used the developer satisfaction scores as a success metric going forward.
 The results provided valuable input to several standardization and pre-standardization discussions at W3C’s annual TPAC meeting.
 Microsoft used the Web DNA as one of their primary research tools when planning investments in the web platform and the surrounding ecosystem of content and tools (for example  webhint.io), and it directly impacted how they now think about areas like cross-browser testing, legacy browser compatibility, best practices hinting, and more.
  The inaugural results were so useful that in 2020 we decided to run it again, with the same level of collaboration between browser vendors and other stakeholders.
 This year we expanded the survey to include some new questions about accessibility tools and web testing, which were requested by some of the survey stakeholders as key areas of interest that should be explored more this time round. We also hired an experienced data scientist to conduct analysis and employ data science best practices.
 We ran the survey from October 12 through November 2, 2020 and secured a similarly wide distribution of respondents.
 Browser compatibility remains the top pain point, and it is also interesting to note that overall satisfaction with the platform hasn’t changed much, with 77.7% being very satisfied or satisfied with the Web in 2020.
 New for this year are the results of our segmentation analysis of the needs which yielded seven, distinct segments. Each one has wildly different needs that surface as the most frustrating when compared to the overall mean scores:
 Documentation Disciples — Their top frustrations are outdated documentation for frameworks and libraries and outdated documentation for HTML, CSS, and JavaScript, as well as supporting specific browsers.
 Browser Beaters — Their top frustrations are clustered around issues with browser compatibility, design and layout. Like Document Disciples, they also find having to support specific browsers more frustrating than the overall mean.
 Progressive Programmers — Their top frustrations are clustered around lack of APIs, lack of support for Progressive Web Apps (PWAs), and using web technologies. For them, browser related needs were typically less frustrating than the overall mean.
 Testing Technicians — Needs statements relating to testing, whether end-to-end, front-end, or testing across browsers, caused the most frustration for this segment. Their top frustrations are clustered around issues with browser compatibility, design and layout. Like Progressive Pros, this segment also finds browser compatibility needs less frustrating than the overall mean, with the exception of testing across browsers.
 Keeping Currents — The need statements that this segment found most frustrating were keeping up with a large number of new and existing tools and frameworks and keeping up with changes to the web platform. Sticking with the themes from 2019, this segment is concerned with the Pace of Change of the web platform.
 Performance Pushers — Needs statements relating to performance and bugs are the top frustrations for this segment. Needs related to testing were rated as less frustrating than the overall mean, but discovering bugs not caught during testing is higher than the overall mean, though the pinpointing performance issues and implementing performance optimization are the top frustrations.
 Regulatory Wranglers — This is the more eclectic segment, with a bigger assortment of needs rating higher than the overall mean. However, compliance with laws and regulations for managing user data is the most frustrating need. Closely following that are needs relating to security measures with tracking protection, data storage, and authentication causing frustrations.
  We are aiming to follow up on key findings with further research in the next few months. This will involve picking some key areas to focus on, and then performing user interviews and further analysis to allow us to drill down into key areas of frustration to see what the way forward is to mitigating them.
    To get the full report, see  https://insights.developer.mozilla.org/ for HTML and PDF versions, and more besides. The 2019 report and our Browser Compatibility follow-up report are still available if you want to compare and contrast.
title: 2020 MDN Web开发人员需求评估现已推出
title_en: 2020 MDN Web Developer Needs Assessment Now Available

市场监管机构的公告于周三晚到达，此前卖空者Muddy Waters于今年初首次报告涉嫌欺诈。针对这些指控，Luckin在四月份表示将发起内部调查。 SEC在6月份表示将取消Luckin的股份，而在7月，Luckin承认确实做过账本。
 惨败发生仅一年后，Luckin通过在纳斯达克的首次交易筹集了6.51亿美元。该公司成立于2017年10月，是从创业公司到上市公司最快的公司之一。
  SEC公告称，这家初创公司希望在中国占据星巴克相当大的市场份额，据称至少在2019年4月至2020年1月之间创造了超过3亿美元的销售额。发现某些员工试图通过将公司的支出夸大超过1.9亿美元，“创建虚假的运营数据库以及更改会计和银行记录以反映虚假销售”来掩盖欺诈行为。
 Luckin既不承认也不否认在纽约南区法院提起的这些索赔。该和解方案尚需法院批准，向中国证券持有人转移资金需要中国当局的批准。
 去年9月，中国的市场监管机构对Luckin和涉及Luckin欺诈的45家公司处以900万美元的罚款。
 尽管存在欺诈性丑闻，Luckin声称业务依旧。该公司在周三的通知中表示，该公司及其商店的运营目前“稳定且正常”。
 “ Luckin将继续与监管机构合作，并优先考虑合规性。同时，我们的管理层和员工将继续确保公司的稳定运营。” 
今年，卖空者一直在追逐在美国上市的中国公司。 Wolfpack Research的一份报告指责由百度支持的中国主要视频流媒体服务提供商爱奇艺（IQiyi）夸大了其数量，这一说法引发了SEC的调查。 中国的课后辅导公司GSX Techedu，在卖空者Citron Research说该公司捏造了销售数字之后，也受到了SEC的类似调查。  “尽管我们在有效地追究外国发行人及其官员和董事与美国发行人和个人同等程度的责任方面存在挑战，但当外国发行人违反联邦证券法时，我们将继续利用所有可用资源来保护投资者， SEC执法部门主管Stephanie Avakian在监管机构对Luckin的公告中说。
note_en:
The announcement by the market regulator  arrived Wednesday evening, months after short-seller Muddy Waters first reported the alleged fraud early this year. In response to the allegations, Luckin  said in April it would launch an internal probe. In June, the SEC said it  would delist Luckin, and in July, Luckin  admitted it did cook its books.
 The fiasco came only a year after Luckin  raised $651 million through its first time sale on Nasdaq. The company was founded in October 2017, making it one of the fastest companies to go from a startup to a public company.
  The startup, which aspired to take a piece of Starbucks’ sizable market share in China, allegedly fabricated more than $300 million in sales between at least April 2019 through January 2020, said the SEC announcement. Certain employees were found attempting to conceal the fraud by inflating the firm’s expenses by more than $190 million, “creating a fake operations database, and altering accounting and bank records to reflect the false sales.”
 Luckin neither admitted or denied these claims, which were filed in a court in the Southern District of New York. The settlement is subject to court approval and the transfer of funds to security holders will  need approval by Chinese authorities.
 In September, China’s market regulator  fined Luckin and 45 companies involved in Luckin’s frauds a total of $9 million after an investigation revealed the coffee company faked its numbers.
 Despite the fraudulent scandal, Luckin claims business is still as usual. Operations of the firm and its stores are currently “stable and normal,” said the company in a notice on Wednesday.
 “Luckin will continue to cooperate with regulators and prioritize compliance. In the meantime, our management and staff will continue to ensure the firm’s stable operation.”
 Short-sellers have been going after U.S.-listed Chinese firms this year. A  report from Wolfpack Research accused iQiyi, a major Chinese video streaming service backed by Baidu, of inflating its numbers, a claim that  triggered an SEC probe. GSX Techedu, a Chinese after-school tutoring company, was  under a similar SEC investigation after short-seller Citron Research said the company fabricated sales numbers.
 “While there are challenges in our ability to effectively hold foreign issuers and their officers and directors accountable to the same extent as U.S. issuers and persons, we will continue to use all our available resources to protect investors when foreign issuers violate the federal securities laws,” said Stephanie Avakian, director of the SEC’s division of enforcement, in the regulator’s announcement on Luckin.
title: 中国的Luckin Coffee将支付1.8亿美元以解决会计欺诈指控
title_en: China’s Luckin Coffee will pay $180 million to settle accounting fraud charges

AutoLeap是一家成立了六个月的公司，总部位于多伦多，在9月悄悄地筹集了500万美元的种子资金。该公司认为，其团队可以弄清楚如何通过将汽车维修店最终带入21世纪来修复这种破碎的经历。它的主要想法是帮助这些商店以透明，无缝的方式组织业务，安排工作，订购零件，进行数字化检查并为客户开具发票。
 它不是第一个尝试使汽车维修过程现代化的人。总部位于西雅图的成立5年的初创公司Wrench已为此筹集了4,000万美元，而另一家进入市场的RepairSmith是戴姆勒的后盾，这家成立两年的总部位于洛杉矶的汽车维修和保养服务。
  尽管如此，在目前全球汽车维修市场价值7,000亿美元的情况下，显然有多个参与者和一种方法可供选择，而AutoLeap则有一些发展空间。
 首先，它拥有一些有用的联系的投资者基础。领导AutoLeap种子轮融资的Threshold Ventures通过与汽车销售平台Shift的赌注与汽车世界建立了联系，该平台于10月通过反向合并上市。
 AutoLeap（其其他风险投资者包括Maple VC，Liquid2 Ventures，Global Founders Capital和Codename Ventures）也得到了一些知名人士的支持，这些人在AutoLeap进入的世界中一直具有影响力。其中包括：Shift联合创始人乔治·阿里森（George Arison），通用汽车前首席执行官里克·瓦格纳（Rick Wagoner）和普利司通前高管内德·阿吉拉尔（Ned Aguilar）。
 更重要的是，AutoLeap的创始人曾一起工作过一次，以振兴一个笨拙的企业。在启动AutoLeap之前，联合首席执行官Rameez Ansari和Steve Lau曾担任FieldEdge的联合首席执行官四年，该公司是一家SaaS公司，旨在帮助承包商运营小型企业。
 他们两个是在多伦多大学相识的大学朋友，他们并没有创办公司。相反，在Lau获得沃顿商学院的MBA学位而Ansari获得斯坦福的MBA学位之后，他们在筹集了所谓的搜索基金后联手收购，管理和发展了一家被忽视的企业，该基金得到了个人投资者的支持并愿意打赌一个团队会找到一家公司进行购买，经营一段时间，然后以更多的钱出售。 
对于所有参与者来说，这都是富有成效的经验。 Lau说，在斥资2000万美元收购FieldEdge（其软件已经存在30年）之后，Lau和Ansari极大地改善了该公司的产品，以至于它们的收费是该公司先前对其产品收费的7倍。然后，他们在2018年以“超过1亿美元的价格”将其出售给了私募股权公司Advent。
 这是一个可靠的出口。减去2000万美元的投资后，该团队保留了FieldEdge出售交易中剩余收益的30％，其余的则捐给了搜索基金的投资者。
 劳说，即便如此，他和安萨里或许仍会继续与竞争对手“服务提坦”（ServiceTitan）竞争，后者“在筹款方面发了疯”。 （这家成立7年的公司已经从投资者那里筹集了4亿美元。）在ServiceTitan艰巨的作战资金到“这对我们来说是第一次退出，” Lau说，“我们转而退出了。”
 今天，Lau和Ansari都不想使用AutoLeap重复这种情况。的确，尽管Lau表示，由于已经获得了种子资金，该公司现在已经“失望”，并且“不会与投资者进行任何讨论”，但有人认为AutoLeap很快就可以进行有关下一轮的讨论。
 投资者实质上将获得的资金是一个蓬勃发展的软件平台，该平台旨在消除纸质传单，摇摇欲坠的传真机和大量发票—如果它能够说服汽车维修店放慢速度以试用其软件，则必须这样做。
 Lau承认，这并非易事。 “这是一项重要的入职努力，因为您已成为他们业务的命脉。”销售过程还需要说服商店共享其现有数据，并花时间进行有关如何使用它们的培训。
 刘不会坦率地说。他说，招募新客户的时间为一到两周，“一旦他们开始看到价值，他们就会获得'啊哈'式的时刻。”实际上，他说AutoLeap已经与少数商店合作，包括多伦多的几家商店，拉斯维加斯的一家商店和波士顿的另一家商店。 
至于扩张计划，Lau表示，公司的部分种子资金将用于数字营销，但它也严重依赖口口相传。 他说，这有助于将车库通常聚集在任何一个地理区域，他认为这将使AutoLeap迅速普及。  Lau表示，没有足够的数据支持这一假设。 但是，如果AutoLeap成功，很快就会有足够的机会。  从上到左，从左到右：AutoLeap联合执行总裁Rameez Ansari和Steve Lau在这张照片中，由于大流行，Lau很快就自愿将其照片进行了购物。
note_en:
AutoLeap, a six-month-old, Toronto-based startup that quietly raised $5 million in seed funding in September, thinks its team can figure out how to repair that broken experience by bringing car repair shops into the 21st century at long last. Its big idea is to help such shops organize their operations, schedule jobs, order parts, conduct digital inspections, and invoice customers in a transparent and seamless way.
 It’s not the first to try to modernize the car repair process. A five-year-old, Seattle-based startup, Wrench, has already raised  $40 million toward that end, while another entrant,  RepairSmith, a two-year-old, L.A.-based car repair and maintenance service, is backed by Daimler.
  Still, with a global automotive repair market that’s currently valued at $700 billion, there’s clearly room for more than one player and one approach, and AutoLeap has a few things going for it.
 For starters, it has an investor base with some useful connections. Threshold Ventures, which led AutoLeap’s seed round, has ties to the automotive world, including through its bet on the car-selling platform  Shift,   which  went public in October via a reverse merger.
 AutoLeap — whose other venture investors include Maple VC, Liquid2 Ventures,  Global Founders Capital,   and Codename Ventures — is also backed by some notable individuals that hold sway in the world that AutoLeap is entering. Among them: Shift cofounder George Arison, former General Motors CEO Rick Wagoner, and former senior Bridgestone exec Ned Aguilar.
 More importantly, AutoLeap’s founders worked together once before to reinvigorate a stodgy business. Before launching AutoLeap, co-CEOs Rameez Ansari and Steve Lau spent four years as the co-CEOs of  FieldEdge, a SaaS company that helps contractors to run their small businesses.
 They two — college friends who’d met at the University of Toronto — didn’t start the company. Rather, after Lau nabbed an MBA from Wharton for Ansari nabbed one from Stanford, they joined forces to acquire, manage and grow a neglected business after raising a a so-called  search fund, a vehicle that’s backed by individual investors willing to bet that a team will find a company to buy, run it for some period of time, then sell it for far more money.
 It was a productive experience for all involved. After spending $20 million to acquire FieldEdge, whose software had already been around for 30 years, Lau and Ansari so dramatically improved the company’s offerings that they were able to charge seven times what the company had previously charged for its products, says Lau. Then they  sold it to the private equity firm Advent in 2018 for “north of $100 million,” says Lau.
 It was a solid exit. Minus that $20 million investment, the team kept 30 percent of the remaining proceeds from FieldEdge’s sale, with the rest going to the search fund’s investors.
 Even so, says Lau, he and Ansari might have kept going if not for rival  ServiceTitan, which “went crazy on the fundraising front.” (The seven-year-old company has raised $400 million altogether from investors.) Between ServiceTitan’s daunting war chest and “given this was a first exit for us,” Lau says, “we transitioned out instead.”
 Today, neither Lau nor Ansari wants to repeat the scenario with AutoLeap. Indeed, though Lau says the company is “heads down” and “not in any discussions” with investors now that it has secured seed funding, one imagines it won’t take long for AutoLeap to enter into discussions about that next round.
 What investors would be funding essentially is a burgeoning software platform that aims to kill off paper flyers, crumbling fax machines, and sheaves of invoices — if only it can convince car repair shops to slow down long enough to try its software.
 It isn’t a no-brainer that they will, admits Lau. “It’s a material onboarding effort, because you become the lifeblood of their business.” The sales process also requires convincing the shops to share their existing data and take the time required to be trained on how to use it.
 Lau isn’t dissuaded, plainly. He says the time to onboard a new customer is one to two weeks and that “once they start seeing value, they get that ‘aha’ type of moment.” In fact, he says that  AutoLeap is already working with a handful of shops, including several in Toronto, one in Las Vegas, and another in Boston.
 As for its expansion plans, Lau says that some of the company’s seed funding will go toward digital marketing but that it’s also relying heavily on word of mouth. It helps, he says, that garages are often clustered in any one geographic area, which he believes will enable AutoLeap to spread quickly.
 There isn’t “a lot of data” to support that assumption, offers Lau. But if AutoLeap has its way, there will be soon enough.
 Above, left to right: AutoLeap co-CEOs Rameez Ansari and Steve Lau in a photo that Lau readily volunteers was Photoshopped owing to the pandemic.
title: AutoLeap表示它将修复您与汽车商店的糟糕关系
title_en: AutoLeap says it will repair your lousy relationship with car shops

note:
英特尔正在通过其SSD P5800X将Optane提升到一个新的水平，该产品被誉为世界上最快的SSD。下一代Optane SSD利用PCIe 4.0接口，第二代Optane介质和新型SSD控制器来提供真正令人震惊的性能和耐用性规格。
  与第一代Optane DC P4800X相比，英特尔表示5800X在随机读/写工作负载方面的性能提高了3倍，达到了150万个4K随机IOPS的峰值，而在7.2 GB / s的峰值上则提高了3倍。
  该驱动器还可以在随机512B工作负载中提供460万IOPS（对于某些类型的缓存工作负载有用），在混合工作负载中提供180万IOPS。
 此外，该驱动器的写入耐久性比第一代P4800X高出67％，现在达到每天惊人的100个驱动器写入（DWPD）的峰值。这也使其成为市场上最耐用的SSD。从角度来看，＆reg;＆reg;基于NAND的SSD甚至提供10DWPD的耐用性，这意味着P5800X确实属于同类产品。
 如果英特尔遵循其标准做法，我们应该会看到这些SSD的消费者版本进入市场，让我们看看性能如何。
    该驱动器利用了英特尔的第二代Optane介质，该介质具有两个卡座/层而不是一个卡座/层，以及一个新的PCIe 4.0 x4 SSD控制器，该控制器最终使该公司摆脱了PCIe 3.0接口的束缚。
 英特尔不会在P5800X的新型PCIe 4.0 SSD控制器后面共享更详细的技术规格。尽管如此，我们知道这些驱动器将采用经过实践检验的U.2外形规格，分别具有400GB，800GB，1.6TB和3.2TB的容量，标志着正常的容量增长是以前存储容量的两倍以上。 -gen DC P4800X。 
与其他Optane驱动器一样，奇特的基础3D XPoint技术提供了比任何基于NAND的SSD更高的速度和耐用性，并提供了介于闪存SSD和系统内存之间的性能水平。与竞争对手的基于NAND的SSD相比，该驱动器还旨在提供更高的服务质量（QoS），这意味着性能一致性是重点关注的领域。英特尔的第一代P4800X已经确立了自己为市场上最一致的SSD的地位，但是英特尔表示P5800X可以在4K随机读取工作负载中将QoS提升40％。
  Optane SSD的最有价值的方面之一就是即使在重负载下也能提供极其一致的性能。正如上面的幻灯片中概述的那样，P5800X在重负载下以超过8 GB / s的吞吐率达到了新的高度，在这个对数规模上轻松超越了所有基于NAND的SSD，并且在其上一代同类产品上也取得了实质性领先。英特尔声称，该驱动器可以提供不到66us的99.999％延迟，这比第一代DC P4800X（更不用说标准SSD）迈出了一大步。
  Optane内存P5800X甚至可以超过我们通常归类为PCIe 4.0总线的吞吐量限制。这是驱动器利用PCIe双向接口能力的副产品。通过以全线速读写性能饱和接口的两侧，该驱动器在混合工作负载期间可提供更高的性能。我们可以在上面概述的70/30读/写工作负载中看到该功能，其中SSD达到了200万IOPS的峰值。在混合的随机工作负载中，这使该驱动器提供的性能比上一代模型高3.7倍。
 英特尔尚未发布价格信息，但该公司表示，驱动器已经交付给OEM公司。自然，作为基于标准的NVMe块可寻址设备，这些SSD也将与AMD的EPYC处理器兼容。
 在性能方面的另一端，英特尔今天还宣布了新的QLC SSD，并计划在不久的将来首次推出每单元5位PLC闪存。您可以在这里阅读有关内容。
      出来的时候我拿起了900便士。随机4k读取测试的速度约为crystaldiskmark中的ramdisk的1/5，而最快的nand则为5x。从理论上讲，ramdisk应该会更快，也许我的cpu会阻止它-2400c10、5775c oc。同样，文件系统（希望它将开始被直接存储之类的东西所绕开）将驱动器性能提升到cpu性能。在日常工作中，我认为与sand nand并没有太大区别。这种新驱动器的性能将更接近ramdisk。并且，直到现代软件能够利用它，您才会注意到日常任务的改进甚至更少。我希望直接存储可采用optane设置。
   回复 
我也有类似的经历，尽管我可以说每天的工作有所不同。 我现在有905p 1.5TB，只是替换了RAID0中的一组PCIe 4.0 Sabrent Rockt m.2。 老实说，这就像白天和黑夜。 我感到震惊。 这必须是存储的未来。 我仍将m.2驱动器用于其他共享媒体存储，但是就应用程序和游戏而言，这是一项了不起的技术。  回复
note_en:
Intel is taking Optane to the next level with its SSD P5800X, which it bills as the fastest SSD in the world. The next-gen Optane SSD leverages the  PCIe 4.0 interface, second-gen Optane media, and a new SSD controller to deliver truly astounding performance and endurance specifications.
  Compared to the  first-gen Optane DC P4800X, Intel says the 5800X offers an impressive 3X performance improvement in random read/write workloads, peaking at 1.5 million 4K random IOPS, and 3X more sequential performance at a peak of 7.2 GB/s.
  The drive also delivers up to 4.6 million IOPS in random 512B workloads (useful for certain types of caching workloads) and up to 1.8 million IOPS in mixed workloads.
 Additionally, the drive offers up to 67% more write endurance than the first-gen P4800X, and now peaks at a staggering 100 drive writes per day (DWPD). That also makes it the most endurant SSD on the market. For perspective, there are very few 'regular' NAND-based SSDs that offer even 10DWPD of endurance, meaning the P5800X is truly in a class of its own.
 If Intel follows its standard practice, we should see consumer variants of these SSDs come to market, let's see what performance looks like.
    The drive leverages  Intel's second-gen Optane media, which comes with two decks/layers instead of one, and a new PCIe 4.0 x4 SSD controller that allows the company to finally  break the shackles of the PCIe 3.0 interface.
 Intel isn't sharing the finer-grained technical specifications behind the P5800X's new PCIe 4.0 SSD controller. Still, we know that the drives will come in the tried-and-true U.2 form factor in capacities of 400GB, 800GB, 1.6TB, and 3.2TB, marking a healthy capacity increase that is more than twice the storage of the previous-gen DC P4800X.
 Just like any other Optane-powered drive, the exotic underlying 3D XPoint technology provides far more speed and endurance than any NAND-based SSD, delivering a level of performance that lands between flash SSDs and system memory. The drives are also designed to offer a superior quality of service (QoS) over competing NAND-based SSDs, meaning performance consistency is a key focus area. Intel's first-gen P4800X had already established itself as the most consistent SSD on the market, but Intel says the P5800X extends that with up to a 40% better QoS in 4K random read workloads.
  Perhaps one of the most valuable aspects of an Optane SSD is its ability to deliver tremendously consistent performance even while it is under heavy load. As outlined in the slide above, the P5800X reaches new heights with more than 8 GB/s of throughput while under heavy load, easily outclassing all NAND-based SSDs on this logarithmic scale and also notching a substantial lead over its previous-gen counterpart. Intel claims the drive can deliver less than 66us of 99.999th percentile latency, which is a massive step forward over the first-gen DC P4800X, not to mention standard SSDs.
  The Optane Memory P5800X can even outstrip what we typically classify as the throughput limit of the PCIe 4.0 bus. That comes as a byproduct of the drive's ability to leverage PCIe's bi-directional interface. By saturating both sides of the interface at full line-rate read and write performance, the drive delivers even higher performance during mixed workloads. We can see that ability in the 70/30 read/write workload outlined above where the SSD peaked at 2 million IOPS. This allows the drive to deliver 3.7x more performance than the previous-gen model in mixed random workloads.
 Intel hasn't released pricing information yet, but the company says the drives are already shipping to OEM companies. Naturally, as a standards-based NVMe block-addressable device, these SSDs will also be compatible with AMD's EPYC processors.
 On the other end of the performance spectrum, Intel also announced new QLC SSDs today, along with its plans to debut 5-bit-per-cell PLC flash, a first, in the near future. You can  read about that here.
      I picked up the 900p when it came out. Random 4k read tested about 1/5 as fast as a ramdisk in crystaldiskmark and 5x as fast as fastest nand. The ramdisk theoretically should have been faster, maybe my cpu is holding it back - 2400c10, 5775c oc. Also the filesystem (which will hopefully start to get bypassed more by stuff like directstorage) is leashing drive performance to cpu performance. I don't see that much of a difference over sata nand in everyday tasks.   This new drive will be even closer to ramdisk in performance. And until modern software takes advantage of it, you will notice the improvement in everyday tasks even less. I hope directstorage comes with an optane setting.
   Reply
 I've had a similar experience, although I can tell there's a difference with daily tasks. I have the 905p 1.5TB now and just replaced a set of PCIe 4.0 Sabrent rockt m.2's in a RAID0. It feels like night and day honestly. I was shocked. This has to be the future of storage. I'm still using the m.2 drives for other shared media storage, but in terms of applications and games, this is amazing tech.
   Reply
title: 英特尔首次展示“最快的固态硬盘” PCIe 4.0 Optane SSD P5800X
title_en: Intel Debuts 'Fastest SSD,' the PCIe 4.0 Optane SSD P5800X

12月5日，一个在保守派社交网络Parler上称其为副总统迈克·彭斯（Mike Pence）的“官方简介”的帐户发布了指向特朗普挑战硬币赠品的链接。
 “与特朗普总统的纪念章一起拥有一段历史！” @MikePenceVicePresident说，将人们引导到一个网站，如果他们为运费付款，他们可以订购免费的特朗普纪念币。该帖子吸引了170,000多次浏览。几天后，Pence帐户转发了一个来自“ Team Trump” Parler帐户的消息，该消息宣传了CBD油，该油错误地声称得到了第一夫人Melania Trump的认可。
 便士办公室确认该帐户是伪造的，该帐户吸引了成千上万的观看次数。假扮杰出的共和党人的大约50个其他Parler帐户，包括白宫办公厅主任Mark Meadows，参议员Lindsey Graham，Donald Trump Jr.和前特朗普律师Sidney Powell，向Parler的亲特朗普用户群出售粗略产品。
 Parler周二表示，在BuzzFeed新闻与该公司联系后，该帐户被禁止。 “我相信这些欺诈性帐户中的大多数都是绕过我们广告网络的可悲尝试，” Parler首席执行官约翰·马特泽（John Matze）说。
 伪造的账户伪装成受欢迎的右翼人物，还有保守的新闻来源和普通的特朗普粉丝，目的是赚钱。他们迅速吸引追随者并获得数十万意见的能力表明了Parler当前的增长突飞猛进，自由奔放，反审查的精神如何为操纵和财务计划创造了机会。得益于参议员特德·克鲁兹（Ted Cruz），众议员德文·尼恩斯（Devin Nunes）和其他共和党人的推广，该平台经历了快速发展，将其定位为不受审查的地方。 《华盛顿邮报》报道说，连同勒索特朗普球迷的假账一样，帕勒由色情垃圾邮件制造者所淹没。帕勒由亿万富翁默瑟家族资助，后者资助了右翼新闻网站Breitbart和不受欢迎的数据公司Cambridge Analytica。
 斯坦福大学天文台的研究经理RenéeDiResta说，Parler已经达到了值得发送垃圾邮件的规模。 “开展这项运动的人利用了一些相当典型的比喻，即自由派精英将保守派从保守派手中夺走，并带有一定的愤慨，认为自由派会做这些事情，这似乎引起了普通Parler用户的共鸣，以回应他们的垃圾邮件，她告诉BuzzFeed新闻。
 帕勒（Parler）首席政策官艾米·佩科夫（Amy Peikoff）表示，该平台试图防止假账户和机器人，并指出它们存在于所有社交平台上。 ＆＃34;我们会指出与竞争对手相比，我们的竞争对手定期定期出现比我们更大，更危险的僵尸程序/虚假帐户大量涌入，并且他们的行为比我们做得少的情况，因此可以回答有关Parler上虚假帐户的所有主张，相对而言，这可以减少假帐户的增长。”她告诉BuzzFeed新闻。 “我们对冒名顶替者帐户的问题持认真态度并认识到，我们将迅速消除任何不符合我们社区准则的问题。” 
伪造的特朗普网络中的账户紧随其后，扩大了彼此的内容，并经常宣传特朗普币，特朗普帽和CBD石油的报价。目前尚不清楚谁负责这些帐户，Parler拒绝透露细节。该网络的核心是一个名为@LibertyWriters的Parler帐户，该帐户在其生物中声称由运营Facebook于2017年禁止的保守网站Liberty Writers News的同一人经营。2018年，BuzzFeed News透露Liberty Writers创始人巴黎·韦德（Wade）和本·高曼（Ben Goldman）与美国第一个政治网站的创建者合作，该网站始于北马其顿，巴尔干这个小国成为了亲特朗普的家庭手工业的所在地。
 韦德（Wade）的一位发言人告诉BuzzFeed新闻，他和高盛（Goldman）与Liberty Writers Parler账户以及其他假货无关。发言人提供了韦德在11月15日发送给Parler的电子邮件副本，以抱怨该帐户是假的。他还从内华达州国务卿发送了一份文件，显示自由作家不再是一家活跃的公司。
 “此页面假装是我拥有的公司。韦德（Wade）在谈到Liberty Writers Parler帐户时写道。韦德和高盛现在经营一家政治咨询公司。
 “自由作家不再是韦德先生经营的财产。瓦德（Wade）发言人肖恩·沃尔​​什（Sean Walsh）表示，它已于10个月前由内华达州国务卿办公室解散。 “当发现某人在Parler上使用该名称时，Parler收到通知，Liberty Writers这个名称与Wade先生无关，该公司应采取相应行动。”
 这些伪造的帐户通常使用与他们复制的经过验证的Twitter帐户相同的个人资料照片和生物文字，并使用多种方法诱使Parler上的人们认为它们是真实的。其中包括利用Parler的验证系统并在验证后更改帐户名称。许多帐户完成了Parler的身份验证过程，该过程要求控制帐户的人上载政府ID。完成后，该帐户会收到一个红色的“ Parler citizen”徽章，该徽章传达了真实性。但是Parler上的人可能没有意识到这仅是确认一个真实的人在该帐户的背后-这并不意味着该帐户的名称与ID上的名称相同。只有具有Parler的“经过验证的影响者”金牌的帐户才能被确认由同一个人或实体运营。
 Peikoff表示，该平台正在“完善”其验证系统。
 她说：“我们的目标只是让人们知道他们何时与真实人（而不是机器人）进行交互，而我们的证章系统（尤其是我们的红色徽章）正是出于这一目的而设计的，”她说。 “我们目前正在完善我们的证章系统，以使其更有效，并解决垃圾邮件发送者最近尝试利用我们的验证程序进行的尝试。” 
DiResta说，Parler的当前系统令人困惑，而且有人可以轻松更改帐户名称，这使其易于操作。她说：“入职时并没有明确区分红色与金色。” “大多数加入社交平台的人都不会考虑可以操纵的所有不同漏洞，比如一个人获得经过验证的徽章然后更改其名字。”
    冒名顶替者Sidney Powell的帐户在个人资料照片中加入了经过Parler认证的假金牌后，就更进一步了。该帐户将自己设为私人帐户，并告诉人们访问鲍威尔法院案件的最新信息的唯一方法是遵循该帐户。 “信息和我所拥有的证据不会为了所有人的利益而公开展示。” “这就是为什么我将帐户设为私有的原因。”
 12月4日，它要求追随者“购买”并通过购买特朗普硬币来帮助佐治亚州决赛中的共和党候选人。硬币网页通过众议院领袖南希·佩洛西（Nancy Pelosi）的虚假推文和福克斯新闻主持人珍妮·皮罗（Jeanine Pirro），娄·多布斯（Lou Dobbs）等人的虚假背书，链接到了被误导的潜在买家。福克斯新闻发言人向BuzzFeed新闻证实，其主持人和人物没有认可该产品。
 DiResta说，Parler需要更加主动地消除垃圾邮件，否则就有疏远用户的风险。她说：“利用假冒和其他掠夺性手段欺骗性垃圾邮件会欺骗用户并骗取金钱，这对Parler品牌或用户体验不利，”她说。
note_en:
On Dec. 5, an account that said it was Vice President Mike Pence’s “Official Profile” on the conservative social network Parler posted a link to a Trump Challenge Coin giveaway.
 “Own A Piece Of History With President Trump’s Commemorative Medallion!” said @MikePenceVicePresident, directing people to a website where they could order a free Trump commemorative coin if they paid for shipping. The post attracted over 170,000 views. Days later, the Pence account reshared a message from a “Team Trump” Parler account that promoted a CBD oil that  falsely claimed to be endorsed by first lady Melania Trump.
 Pence’s office confirmed that the account, which attracted hundreds of thousands of views, is fake. So are roughly 50 other Parler accounts that masqueraded as prominent Republicans, including White House chief of staff Mark Meadows, Sen. Lindsey Graham, Donald Trump Jr., and former Trump attorney Sidney Powell to shill sketchy products to Parler’s pro-Trump user base.
 On Tuesday, Parler said it banned the accounts after being contacted by BuzzFeed News. “I believe most of those fraudulent accounts were a sad attempt to circumvent our advertising network,” Parler CEO John Matze said.
 The fake accounts masqueraded as popular right-wing figures, as well as conservative news sources and average Trump fans, with the goal of earning money. Their ability to quickly attract followers and hundreds of thousands of views shows how Parler’s current  growth spurt and freewheeling, anti-censorship ethos has created opportunities for manipulation and financial schemes. The platform experienced rapid growth thanks to promotion from  Sen. Ted Cruz,  Rep. Devin Nunes, and other Republicans who pitch it as a place free from censorship. Along with fake accounts preying on Trump fans, the Washington Post  reported that Parler, which is funded by the billionaire Mercer family who bankrolled right-wing news site Breitbart and disgraced data firm Cambridge Analytica, is inundated by porn spammers.
 Renée DiResta, research manager of the Stanford Internet Observatory, said Parler has reached the size where it's worth spamming. “The people who ran this campaign leveraged some pretty typical tropes about liberal elites taking things away from conservatives, framed with the requisite outrage that liberals would do such things, and it seems that it resonated enough for ordinary Parler users to echo their spam posts,” she told BuzzFeed News.
 Amy Peikoff, Parler’s chief policy officer, said the platform tries to prevent fake accounts and bots, and noted that they exist on all social platforms. "We would answer any and all claims about fake accounts on Parler by noting that our competitors experience a much larger, more dangerous influx of bots/fake accounts on a regular basis than we do, and they do less than we do, relatively speaking, to curtail the growth of fake accounts,” she told BuzzFeed News. “We are conscientious and cognizant of the problem of impersonator accounts, and we are quick to eliminate any which do not comply with our community guidelines.”
    The accounts in the faux Trump network followed each other, amplified each other’s content, and often promoted offers for Trump coins, Trump hats, and CBD oil. It’s unclear who operated the accounts, and Parler declined to release details. At the heart of the network was a Parler account called @LibertyWriters, which claimed in its bio to be run by the same people who operated Liberty Writers News,  a conservative site that Facebook  banned in 2017. In 2018, BuzzFeed News  revealed that Liberty Writers founders Paris Wade and Ben Goldman worked with the creators of the first American politics website run from North Macedonia, the small Balkan country that became home to  a cottage industry of pro-Trump sites.
 A spokesperson for Wade told BuzzFeed News that he and Goldman had nothing to do with the Liberty Writers Parler account and the other fakes. The spokesperson provided a copy of an email Wade sent to Parler on Nov. 15 to complain that the account was fake. He also sent a document from the Nevada secretary of state showing that Liberty Writers is no longer an active company.
 “This page is pretending to be a company I own. We aren’t running it,” wrote Wade about the Liberty Writers Parler account. Wade and Goldman now run a political consulting firm.
 “Liberty Writers is no longer a property operated by Mr. Wade. It was dissolved with the Nevada secretary of state’s office more than 10 months ago,” said Sean Walsh, a spokesperson for Wade. “When it was discovered that some group was using the name on Parler, Parler was notified that the name Liberty Writers was not affiliated with Mr. Wade and the company should act accordingly.”
 The fake accounts, which often used the same profile photos and bio text as the verified Twitter accounts they duplicated, used a variety of methods to trick people on Parler into thinking they were authentic. This included exploiting Parler’s verification system and changing account names after getting verified. Many of the accounts completed Parler’s identification verification process, which requires the person controlling an account to upload a government ID. Once they do, the account receives a red “Parler citizen” badge that conveys an added layer of authenticity. But people on Parler are likely unaware that this only confirms a real person is behind the account — it does not mean that the account’s name is the same as on the ID. Only accounts with Parler’s gold “verified influencer” badge are confirmed to be run by the same person or entity.
 Peikoff said the platform is in the process of “refining” its verification system.
 “Our goal has been simply to let people know when they are interacting with real people, not bots, and our badging system — particularly our red badge — was designed with this purpose in mind,” she said. “We are currently refining our badging system to make it more effective at this, and to address recent attempts by spammers to game our verification processes.”
 DiResta said Parler’s current system is confusing, and the ease with which someone can change account names leaves it open to manipulation. “The red versus gold distinction wasn't really made clear upon onboarding,” she said. “Most people who join social platforms aren't thinking about all of the different loopholes that can be manipulated, like a person getting a verified badge and then changing their name.”
    The imposter Sidney Powell account went a step further by including a fake gold Parler verified influencer badge in its profile photo. The account made itself private and told people the only way to access the latest information about Powell’s court cases was to follow it. “The information & evidence I have is not to be displayed publicly for the good of all,” read the account’s bio. “That’s why I turned my account private.”
 On Dec. 4, it asked followers to “chip in” and help the Republican candidates in the Georgia runoff by buying a Trump coin. The  coin webpage it linked to misled potential buyers with a fake tweet from House Leader Nancy Pelosi and fake endorsements from Fox News hosts Jeanine Pirro, Lou Dobbs, and others. A Fox News spokesperson confirmed to BuzzFeed News that its hosts and personalities did not endorse the products.
 DiResta said Parler needs to become more proactive about removing spam or it risks alienating users. “Deceptive spam rings using impersonation and other predatory practices to deceive users and bilk them of money is not going to be good for the Parler brand or user experience,” she said.
title: 假GOР领导人在Parler上出售CBD油 大约有50个虚假帐户利用保守的社交网络骗取了特朗普的硬币，特朗普的帽子和CBD油。

保护自己的互联网安全的首要规则是确保您的密码不容易被猜中。但看起来很重要的一个人没有遵循这个建议：唐纳德·特朗普总统。
 荷兰媒体报道说，十月份，一名黑客通过猜测自己的密码进入了特朗普的Twitter帐户。而且，我不骗你，密码是“ maga2020！”。 -当然是。
 尽管白宫和推特坚持认为没有任何黑客入侵的证据，但荷兰检察官周三证实了入侵的细节。这位44岁的黑客Victor Gevers因访问总统臭名昭著的社交媒体帐户而面临潜在的入狱时间。但是检察官说，盖弗斯以“道德”的方式行事，立即向荷兰当局披露了他的所作所为。
 荷兰检察官办公室周三表示：“我们相信，黑客实际上已经侵入了特朗普的Twitter帐户，但符合判例法制定的标准，可以免费成为一名道德黑客。”
 盖弗斯说，他在美国总统大选之前一直在寻找备受关注的社交媒体帐户中的漏洞，以防万一它们被有恶意的人入侵。这是一件好事，特别是因为特朗普显然没有在他的手机上激活两因素身份验证，这将在密码之外增加了额外的安全性步骤。幸运的是，Gevers在其他人之前发现了一个问题。荷兰警方在两个月前谈到盖弗斯的行为时说：“黑客本人释放了登录名。”
  只能希望这会导致特朗普的帐户比以前更安全，尤其是在Twitter要求九月份主要政客提供更可靠的密码之后。只要没有人尝试使用“ StopTheSteal2020！”登录即可。

note_en:
The first rule of securing yourself on the internet is to ensure your password isn’t easily guessable. But it looks like someone pretty important didn’t follow that advice: President Donald Trump.
 Dutch media reported that in October, a hacker got into Trump’s Twitter account by guessing his password. And, I kid you not, the password was “maga2020!” — because of course it was.
 Despite  insistence from the White House and Twitter that there was no evidence of a hack, public  prosecutors in the Netherlands confirmed details of an intrusion on Wednesday. The hacker, 44-year-old  Victor Gevers, was facing potential jail time for accessing the president’s infamous social media account. But prosecutors said Gevers had acted in an “ ethical” way by immediately disclosing what he had done to Dutch authorities.
 “We believe the hacker has actually penetrated Trump’s Twitter account, but has met the criteria that have been developed in case law to go free as an ethical hacker,” the  Netherlands’ public prosecutor office said on Wednesday.
 Gevers said he was looking for vulnerabilities in high-profile social media accounts ahead of the US presidential election, just in case they got hacked by someone with malicious intent. That’s a good thing, especially since Trump apparently didn’t have  two-factor authentication activated on his phone, which would have added an extra security step besides a password. Luckily, Gevers found a problem before someone else did. “The hacker released the login himself,”  Dutch police said about Gevers’s actions two month ago.
  One can only hope this led to Trump’s account being made more secure than it had been, especially after  Twitter required more robust passwords from leading politicians in September. As long as nobody tries logging in using “StopTheSteal2020!”

在作品中对角色进行单个（通常是次要的）动作或特征，并随着时间的推移逐渐夸大它，直到完全消耗了角色的行为。大多数情况下，特质/动作变得完全古怪，成为其定义特征。 Sitcoms和Sitcom角色特别容易受到这种影响，长期演出中的外围角色也是如此。
 这部戏曲以《辛普森一家》中的人物内德·佛兰德斯（Ned Flanders）的名字命名，他最初被描绘成一个友好，慷慨的基督徒邻居以及模范的父亲，丈夫和公民，因此使他与荷马·辛普森（Homer Simpson）形成鲜明对比。随着季节的发展，他变得越来越迷恋宗教，以至于他最终体现了敬畏上帝，重击圣经的美国基督教传教士的几乎所有负面定型观念。
  请注意，此单反的关键在于过程是如何渐进的：角色开始时是相对正常的，有一些怪癖，怪癖变得更加突出，然后怪癖逐渐成为角色。如果只是在作者知道如何处理角色之前就与角色有何不同，那就是“表征前进”。

   发生在Alan Partridge中的几个字符：Alpha Papa。戴夫·克利夫顿（Dave Clifton）从艾伦（Alan）关于自己所谓的酗酒的恶作剧开了一个不那么显眼的省级DJ，到欣喜地公开叙述他在酒，可卡因，海洛因和妓女方面的经历。琳恩（Lynn）以前几乎没有提到她的基督教，但突然变得更加公开地信仰宗教。奇怪的是，艾伦本人却遇到了相反的情况，在电影中变得不那么鲜明，而且角色更加普遍。
 外国人的Weyland-Yutani公司。每部电影，书本和游戏似乎都夸大了他们尝试利用异形来获取利润的尝试。在第一部电影中，他们了解异形，并尝试利用Nostromo的工作人员将其带回地球进行研究。除此之外，他们似乎并不特别着迷。他们只是一些可能有价值的生命形式。 Weyland-Yutani看了八部电影以及大量的漫画和视频游戏之后，就沉迷于研究异国情调并从中获利，以致自杀的边缘化，以至于您开始怀疑该公司在仅进行项目开发时，甚至是如何获利的似乎正在与外星虫子纠缠不清。他们的不道德行为也越来越被夸大了。在第一部电影中，他们愿意牺牲一小撮光荣的卡车司机来抓捕外星人，而后来的作品则表明，他们在狩猎时牺牲了大量的荒谬人员。为了研究实际上似乎没有任何实际应用的动物，很容易就算出被摧毁了多少个殖民地和研究设施。
 恐怖经典《儿童游戏》的前两部续集让查基fl然成为一个愚蠢的邪恶小人，他极其无能，并且从未实现他的主要目标，而是浪费时间去杀杀色情狂，而不是拥有安迪。恐怖喜剧引起了整个杀手娃娃概念的嘲笑，这可能是因为作家意识到特许经营变得多么愚蠢。但是随后出现了“查基的诅咒”，这使查基重新回到了正轨。
 尽管很少有人会考虑妖精的电影杰作，但第一部电影至少是一部直刻的电影，带有一些漫画浮雕。即使到那时，大多数幽默还是视线堵塞或粗俗的双关语。在第二部分中，该系列开始增强幽默感和自我意识，以至于超越了恐怖元素。在随后的续集中，妖精模仿了猫王和约翰·韦恩，完全用押韵说话，变成了狂饮和抽烟的人，最令人讨厌的是说唱。
